  • locate-sublime-projects-clistars0 - Allows searching for Sublime Text projects and opens them in Sublime.
  • loremipsumstars0 - Generate lorem ipsum text on the command line. Gets its data from lipsum.com.
  • lumberjackstars35 - Lumberjack is a logging interface for shell scripts.
  • mac-packagingstars1 - A set of common functions used for enterprise Mac packaging with Munki.
  • macos (joow)stars3 - A ZSH plugin for macOS.
  • mage2dockerstars11 - Makes it easy to work with Docker and Magento 2. Speeds up and simplifies common commands like clean cache, setup upgrade, compile di and much more in Magento 2 on containers.
  • magento-2stars25 - Adds m2 function to run magento binary, adds tab completions.
  • magic-enterstars3 - Make your enter key magical by binding a ZSH command to it.
  • manydots-magicstars38 - A zle tweak for emulating ...'==../..' etc.
  • markeditstars5 - Mark files and edit them with autocompletion for existing marks.
  • markgatestars4 - Allows you to mark directories so you can jump directly to them.
  • mfuncstars6 - Allows you to define persistent functions on-the-fly, without the need to add them to your config files. These functions are permanently available until you delete them.
  • mode-switch.CLIstars3 - A ZSH plugin for switching command line between normal mode and vi mode.
  • monorepo-pluginstars2 - ZSH plugin for monorepo management.
  • monthrenamestars1 - Renames month names to numbers in filenames.
  • mouse-statusstars2 - Changes mouse color based on status code, uses libratbag.
  • mylocationstars9 - A plugin to show your current location based on your IP address.
  • nstars9 - Auto-switches node versions based on project environment using nstars16.9k.
  • namelinkstars0 - Provides an automatically synchronized mapping of filesystem entries (typically symbolic links) in a set of directories to their counterparts in the named directory hash.
  • navistars0 - Plugin for navistars11.9k.
  • navigation-toolsstars5 - Adds htop-like kill, directory bookmarks browser, a multi-word incremental history searcher and more.
  • new-file-from-templatestars12 - Generates file from template.
  • newvwpstars1 - Spins up a new WordPress site using Valet.
  • nix-shellstars222 - Plugin that lets you use ZSH as the default shell in a nix-shell environment.
  • nnvmstars4 - auto-switches node versions based on .nvmrc. Requires nstars16.9k.
  • node-env-installerstars2 - Uses nvm to install new versions and modules for the current project.
  • nodestars5 - Srijan Shetty's nodejs plugin for ZSH with caching of nvm completions and autoloading of nvm if present.
  • nodenv (jsahlen)stars4 - Auto-load nodenv and its completions into the shell.
  • nodenv (mattberther)stars2 - Installs, updates and loads nodenv. Inspired by zsh-rbenv.
  • nohupstars7 - Add nohup to the current command pressing Ctrl-H.
  • noreallyjustfuckingstopalreadystars293 - macOS users know the pain of trying to figure out what command actually flushes the DNS cache on their version of macOS, and this plugin makes that annoyance go away.
  • notes (aperezdc)stars10 - Inspired by terminal_velocity, it provides a fast interface to create and access a set of Markdown text files inside a directory.
  • notes (chipsenkbeil)stars16 - Provides a quick notes editing experience in ZSH.
  • notify (luismayta)stars8 - Notifications for ZSH with auto installation of dependencies and r2d2 sounds.
  • notify (marzocchi)stars451 - A plugin for ZSH (on macOS and Linux) that posts desktop notifications when a command terminates with a non-zero exit status or when it took more than 30 seconds to complete, if the terminal application is in the background (or the command's terminal tab is inactive).
  • npm (igoradamenko)stars5 - Add npm aliases & command completion. Based on the Oh-My-Zsh npm plugin.
  • npm (trystan2k)stars0 - Adds npm aliases. Based on the Oh-My-Zsh npm plugin.
  • npm (zfben)stars0 - Use n as npm aliases with noglob prefix and more. Based on the Oh-My-Zsh npm plugin.
  • nvm-auto-usestars16 - Calls nvm use automatically whenever you enter a directory that contains an .nvmrc file with a string telling nvm which node to use.
  • nvm-lazystars4 - Plugin for lazy loading of oh-my-zsh's *nvm- plugin. It supports lazy-loading nvm for more than one binary/entrypoint, with the defaults being nvm, node and npm.
  • nvmstars1.9k - ZSH plugin for installing, updating and loading nvm.
  • oathstars6 - Manages 2FA authentication 6 digit tokens. It was highly inspired by this article about using oathtool for 2 step verification.
  • oclif completion generatorstars8 - Generates shell completions for commands lacking them.
  • oh-my-matrixstars19 - Turn your terminal into the matrix.
  • omz-themes-standalonestars4 - Gives you the oh-my-zshstars151k themes without requiring everything else that comes with oh-my-zsh
  • open-create-projectsstars3 - Open/Create projects in Jetbrains.
  • open-prstars63 - A ZSH plugin to open pull requests from command line.
  • openshift-originstars0 - Add a few shortcuts to common openshift origin (oc) actions.
  • opera-git-pluginstars0 - git aliases.
  • operatorstars4 - Clean and simple theme, works best with Menlo for Powerlinestars739.
  • oppstars234 - Vim's text-objects-ish for ZSH.
  • opt-pathstars11 - Automatically add ~/opt subpaths to your $PATH.
  • osx-autoproxystars83 - Configures proxy environment variables based on macOS's system preferences.
  • osx-devstars13 - This plugin adds some commands for maintaining various server programs on a macOS install.
  • osxstars17 - Add some common macOS related aliases and functions.
  • p10k-promptconfigstars2 - adds the ability to easily switch between Powerlevel10k prompt theme config files by defining the P10K_PROMPT variable in your .zshrc file.
  • package-any-nodestars0 - Easy installing of any Node modules inside the plugin directory, exposing their binaries via shims (i.e.: forwarder scripts) created automatically by Bin-Gem-Nodestars4 annex.
  • packerstars1 - Adds aliases and auto-completes for Hashicorp packer.
  • pantheon-terminal-notifystars12 - Background notifications for long running commands. Supports Elementary OS Freya.
  • passwordless-historystars12 - Keeps passwords from entering your command line history.
  • path-ethicstars5 - Helps manage your $PATH quickly and easily. Doesn't touch your existing .zshrc, .zprofile, but adds on top of your existing environment instead.
  • pctlstars8 - Toggle the environment variables for proxying.
  • penteststars39 - Aliases and functions for the lazy penetration tester.
  • per-directory-historystars200 - Per directory history for ZSH, as well as global history, and the ability to toggle between them with ^G.
  • percolstars8 - Interactively and incrementally search history/resume background jobs using percolstars3.1k.
  • perlbrewstars0 - Installs perlbrew if not already installed and initializes it for your shell.
  • pewstars1 - Sets up and manages Python virtualenvs using pewstars1.1k, automatically switches virtualenvs as you move directories.
  • pgstars18 - Adds utility functions to work with PostgreSQL.
  • ph-marksstars9 - Bookmark pornhub videos from your terminal.
  • php-version-rcfile-switcherstars6 - Automatically switch between php versions using php-versionstars666 if an rc-file is present in a directory.
  • phpcsstars2 - Plugin for PHP code snifferstars9.7k.
  • phpenvstars1 - Auto-load phpenvstars0 and its completions.
  • phpunitstars6 - Plugin for PHPUnit.
  • pip-appstars36 - Makes it easy to install python applications into distinct Python virtualenvs so they don't conflict with any other python requirements on your system.
  • pipxstars5 - Autocompletions for pipxstars5.6k.
  • plugin-ibtoolstars1 - Adds ibtool shortcuts to generate localized XIB files.
  • plugin-railsstars2 - ZSH plugin for Rails.
  • plugin-vscodestars16 - Plugin for Visual Studio Code, a text editor for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
  • poetry (darvid)stars53 - Automatically activates and deactivates Poetry-created python virtualenvs.
  • poetry (sudosabin)stars3 - Enables poetry $PATH and autocompletions.
  • posh-git-bashstars344 - Adds git status in your prompt.
  • ppsmonstars0 - Reads /sys/class/net/$interface/ to keep track of packet transmission rates. It stores the current rate to a file in the RAM backed filesystem where it can be easily accessed for display in a shell-prompt. Linux-only due to use of /sys.
  • pr-cwdstars1 - Creates a global variable with current working directory. Plugin has integration with jocelynmallon/zshmarksstars249.
  • pr-eolstars0 - Displays an EOL symbol which can be embedded in the prompt.
  • pr-exec-timestars2 - Adds a variable you can use to display the execution time of the last command run.
  • pr-gitstars1 - Creates a global variable with git status information that can be displayed in prompts.
  • pr-is-rootstars0 - Sets an environment variable you can use in a custom prompt when running as root.
  • pr-jobsstars1 - Creates an environment variable which can be used to display background job information in a custom prompt.
  • pr-nodestars0 - Sets an environment variable which can be used to display nodeJS information in a custom prompt.
  • presenter-modestars2 - Expands aliases during presentations. It also increases the terminal window's contrast to enhance visibility.
  • pretty-time (sindresorhus)stars65 - Convert seconds to a human readable string: 165392 → 1d 21h 56m 32s.
  • pretty-time (zpm-zsh)stars1 - Converts raw seconds into human-readable strings.
  • print-aliasstars14 - Prints commands with aliases expanded whenever you use an alias at the command line.
  • printcstars13 - Allows you to print in any color in the RGB space via a simple printc call.
  • profile-secretsstars22 - Securely keep sensitive variables (api tokens, passwords, etc) as part of your terminal init files. Uses gpg to encrypt/decrypt the file with your secrets.
  • project (voronkovich)stars4 - Plugin for managing projects.
  • prompt-generatorstars2 - Generates custom themes. Some generated themes require powerline-compatible fonts.
  • proxy-pluginstars14 - Quickly enable and disable proxy shell environment settings.
  • proxystars290 - Configure proxy settings for some package managers and software.
  • pyenv-lazy-loadstars3 - Plugin for lazy-loading pyenv in ZSH.
  • q (cal2195)stars58 - Add vim-like macro registers to your ZSH shell.
  • q (tomsquest)stars0 - Tail/remove the temp file for Qstars1.4k, the Dirty Debugging Tool.
  • qiime2stars2 - Adds functions and aliases to make working with Quiime 2 easier.
  • quer-ps1stars0 - A highly extendable manager for your ZSH prompt
  • quoterstars8 - Display a random quote when opening a new terminal session.
  • randemestars4 - Chooses a random theme for each session. If you not like the chosen theme you can run randeme_rm to never show that theme again.
  • random-quotesstars6 - Displays random quotes or facts.
  • razer-status-codestars4 - Change the colour of your Razer Mouse based on the status of the last executed command. Requires OpenRazer linux drivers.
  • rc-filesstars2 - Adds shortcut functions for editing various rc files.
  • recallstars17 - Makes using command history easier.
  • reentry-hookstars5 - Plugin that re-enters working directory if it has been removed and re-created.
  • reloadstars3 - Adds function to quickly reload your .zshrc.
  • reminderstars32 - A plugin which displays reminders above every prompt.
  • replace-multiple-dotsstars1 - Converts ... to ../..
  • revolverstars127 - A progress spinner for ZSH scripts.
  • riddle-mestars1 - Displays random riddles.
  • ripzstars23 - Reminds you of your aliases, so you use them more. Depends on ripgrepstars33.6k.
  • robostars2 - A ZSH plugin for Robostars2.5k.
  • rockzstars8 - Lua + LuaRocks virtual environment manager based upon VirtualZ.
  • safe-pastestars12 - A safe-paste plugin. See Conrad Irwin's bracketed-paste blog post.
  • saml2aws-autostars4 - When using multiple AWS profiles, e.g. different accounts for your stages (development, pre-prod, prod), can be used to determine which profile is currently exported and if the token is still valid.
  • saml2awsstars10 - Add support for saml2awsstars1.7k.
  • sandboxdstars221 - Speed up your .zshrc & shell startup with lazy-loading by only running setup commands (e.g. eval "$(rbenv init -)", etc) when you need them.
  • sb-upgradestars0 - Script to automatically update apps on a seedbox.
  • schrootstars1 - Show current chroot name in your prompt.
  • sdkmanstars2 - Installs sdkman and adds completions and aliases for it.
  • sealionstars9 - Allows you to set reminders that will appear in your terminal when your prompt is refreshed.
  • search-directory-historystars2 - Allows complex search of per-directory history created using the per-directory-historystars200 plugin.
  • sed-substars1 - Adds keybindings to do global search and replace on current command line.
  • selectstars6 - Multi-term searched selection list with approximate matching and uniq mode.
  • sendstars27 - Single command to git add, git commit, and git push for much faster git workflow.
  • sensei-gitstars3 - Adds many git aliases and helper shell functions.
  • setenvstars7 - Runs a script when you change directories.
  • shelfstars4 -Utility which can be used to bookmark and access directly any file using mnemonics.
  • show-pathstars0 - Provides a function shows the $PATH line by line.
  • simpleserver - Plugin to easily start python SimpleHTTPServer and SimpleHTTPSServer.
  • slugifystars3 - Converts filenames and directories to a web friendly format.
  • smart-cdstars17 - Runs ls and git status after chpwd.
  • smartinputstars4 - When you type brackets or quotes, the corresponding end brackets/quotes are automatically added.
  • smilestars1 - Adds function to display random smileys.
  • snippetsstars32 - Command line snippet expansion.
  • solarized-manstars27 - A modified version of oh-my-zsh's plugin colored-man-pages, optimized for the solarized dark theme in the terminal.
  • spackstars2 - Includes some useful aliases and functions for loading/unloading Spackstars3k-generated modules. As it makes use of the module command it is much more efficient than spack load.
  • ssh-connectstars104 - A simple ssh manager.
  • ssh-pluginstars4 - Plugin for ssh.
  • sshukhstars5 - Will update your known_hosts file when you ssh into a server.
  • startifystars1 - Shows recently used vim files, shell-util files, active tmux sessions, recently-run git commands and more.
  • startup-timerstars18 - Print the time it takes for the shell to start up.
  • stashystars3 - Plugin that simplifies using git stash.
  • statifystars3 - Plugin that does basic statistical analysis.
  • suffix-aliasstars4 - Directly open files in the shell using ZSH's suffix aliases.
  • switch-gitstars0 - Easy switching between git repositories. Just type sgr <some part of you repo's name>, press enter and you're there.
  • syntax-highlighting-filetypesstars143 - ZSH syntax highlighting with dircolors in realtime.
  • sys-diverstars7 - A ZSH plugin for directory change or editor startup with only key operations using widgets without typing commands.
  • sysadmin-utilstars901 - Steve Kemp's collection of tool scripts for sysadmins.
  • systemdstars9 - Adds many aliases for systemd.
  • t32stars2 - Plugin for the Lauterbach Trace32 toolset. It automatically registers fonts and sets all necessary environment variables to run the t32 toolset.
  • tab-titlestars32 - Set the terminal tab title according to current directory or running process. Forked from termsupport.zsh
  • tailfstars2 - Adds tailf function with prefixed newlines instead of trailing newlines.
  • taskbookstars3 - Auto-completes task numbers for taskbook.
  • terminal-titlestars3 - Adds a set-term-title function you can use to title terminal windows.
  • terminal-workload-reportstars2 - A plugin that calculates and displays how many commands have been run via terminal.
  • terraform (macunha1)stars12 - Add convenience aliases for terraform, tab completions and helper function to add your terraform workspace in the prompt.
  • terraform (pbar1)stars1 - Terraform convenience functions and aliases for ZSH.
  • terragruntstars4 - Plugin for Terragruntstars6.2k, a thin wrapper for Terraform that provides extra tools.
  • tfswitchstars0 - Installs and loads tfswitchstars1k.
  • tgswitchstars0 - Installs and loads tgswitchstars100.
  • thefuckstars19 - Loads thefuckstars74k (a tool which corrects your previous command) with cache support, which reduces the loading time dramatically.
  • theia-dev-toolsstars0 - Convenience functions for working with theia-idestars17.4k.
  • tigstars1 - Adds a few advanced bindings for tigstars11.2k and also provides a tig-pick script.
  • timewarriorstars39 - Adds support for timewarrior, a time-tracking application.
  • tipzstars24 - Displays your alias if you have an alias for the command you just ran, similarly to alias-tipsstars652.
  • titlestars5 - Allows you to set a terminal window title.
  • titlesstars49 - Automatic window and tab titles for tmux and xterm-compatible terminals.
  • tmstars5 - Simplifies creating new tmux sessions, attaching to existing sessions, switching between sessions, and listing active sessions.
  • tmux-auto-titlestars12 - Automatically sets the title of windows/panes as the current foreground command.
  • tmux-multisessionstars0 - Plugin for tmux to support multiple sessions on a single server process.
  • tmux-renamestars3 - Rename tmux windows automatically.
  • tmux-simplestars5 - Simple plugin for using tmux with ZSH.
  • tmux-vim-integrationstars4 - Open files in a running vim (or NeoVim) session, from an adjacent tmux pane.
  • tmux-zsh-vim-titlesstars27 - Create unified terminal titles for tmux, ZSH, and Vim/NVIM, modular.
  • tmuxstars12 - Plugin for tmux.
  • tmuxreplstars21 - Simple ZSH plugin to have a R-EP-L tmux session.
  • togglstars5 - Adds a toggl-week command to display the total working hours tracked on toggl.com
  • toggle-command-prefixstars13 - Add a widget to toggle a prefix to a command. Binds Alt+s to prefix a command with sudo by default.
  • traistastars0 - Includes git status decorations and color-coded exit status of the last command run. Better with dark terminal themes.
  • trestars0 - Makes using trestars341 easier.
  • tsmstars14 - Adds a tmux Session Manager.
  • tumultstars139 - Adds tools for macOS.
  • ubuntualiasesstars2 - Ubuntu 16 aliases.
  • ugitstars975 - Lets you undo your last git operation.
  • uncloudiumstars0 - Adds helper script to download crx files from the Google Chrome web store.
  • undollarstars12 - Strips the dollar sign from the beginning of the terminal prompt.
  • unix-simplestars0 - A command that shows a graphic about the simplicity of unix.
  • up (cjayross)stars3 - A simple way to navigate up through directories.
  • up (peterhurford)stars34 - Adds an up command to cd multiple levels up.
  • url-highlighterstars28 - A plugin for the ZSH syntax highlighter that turns URLs green if they respond with a "good" status, and red otherwise. Useful for checking URL typos.
  • vagrant-box-wrapperstars2 - A wrapper plugin for vagrant that allows for calling vagrant commands from outside of the box directory. The plugin also ships with a few extra commands that help to manage more than one box, along with custom tab-completion.
  • vanilli.shstars17 - A lightweight start point of shell configuration.
  • vcshrstars0 - Help vcsh users require Github repositories using vcsh for auto-installation in ~/.zshrc, etc.
  • velocitystars3 - Powerline-based theme elements for ZSH and tmux.
  • venv-litestars4 - A super-lightweight sort-of-clone of virtualenvwrapper; it pretty much expects you to be using pyenvstars29k (though you don't *have- to), and because it's based on the venv module, (creation) only works for python >= 3.3.
  • venv-wrapperstars1 - Provides ZSH functions to ease the management of your virtual environments using venv.
  • vi-incrementstars9 - Add vim-like increment/decrement operations.
  • vi-mode (nyquase)stars24 Add extra vi-like functionality.
  • vi-mode (sinetoami)stars0 - Add more vi-like functionality to ZSH.
  • vi-motionsstars28 - Add new motions and text objects including quoted/bracketed text and commands.
  • vi-quotestars5 - Add an operation which quotes or unquotes a motion.
  • viexchangestars10 - A vi mode plugin for easily swapping text between two places in the buffer, like vim-exchange.
  • vim-modestars276 - Friendly vi-mode bindings, adding basic Emacs keys, incremental search, mode indicators and more.
  • vim-pluginstars18 - Allows you to do vim filename:123 to open a file with the cursor at a specific line.
  • vimmanstars18 - View vim plugin manuals (help) like man in ZSH.
  • vimtostars30 - Improved ZSH vi mode (bindkey -v) plugin.
  • virtualzstars9 - Python virtualenv manager inspired by Adam Brenecki's Virtualfishstars952 for the Fish shell, replaces virtualenvwrapper.
  • visitstars1 - Custom plugin for faster navigation.
  • volta (cowboyd)stars5 - Seamlessly install and configure the Volta NodeJS toolchain manager.
  • voltastars2 - Installs and loads Volta: JS Toolchains as Codestars6.8k.
  • vscode (qianxinfeng)stars31 - Plugin for Visual Studio Code.
  • wakatime (sobolevn)stars75 - Track how much time you have spent in your terminal. Has per project stats.
  • wakatime (wbingli)stars117 - Automatic time tracking for commands in ZSH using wakatime.
  • warholstars52 - Configures colorization with grcstars1.5k.
  • watchstars3 - Easily prefix your current or previous commands with watch by pressing CTRL + W.
  • watson.zshstars3 - A plugin for the watsonstars2.1k time management system.
  • wdstars642 - Warp directory lets you jump to custom directories in ZSH, without using cd. Why? Because cd seems inefficient when the folder is frequently visited or has a long path.
  • wdnotestars0 - Prints the contents of a .wdnote file if present when changing to a new directory.
  • web-search (sinetoami)stars2 - Add commands to run bing, google, yahoo, & duckduckgo searches directly from the CLI.
  • web-search (yabanahano)stars0 - Adds aliases for searching with Google, Wiki, Bing, YouTube and other popular services.
  • window-titlestars12 - Adds informative tiles to your terminal windows.
  • windows-titlestars4 - Dynamically updates terminal window title with current directory and the last command run.
  • workonstars6 - Simple utility for jumping between projects.
  • worktreestars2 - Adds functions that wrap git worktree.
  • xxh-plugin-zsh-zshrcstars3 - plugin for xxh-shell-zshstars25 that copies your ~/.zshrc to a remote host and sources it with xxh-shell-zshstars25.
  • you-should-usestars907 - ZSH plugin that reminds you to use those aliases you defined.
  • youtube-dl-aliasesstars3 - Adds yt aliases to download videos from YouTube.
  • youtube-dlstars2 - Simple plugin for youtube-dl.
  • z.luastars2.4k - A command line tool which helps you navigate faster by learning your habits. An alternative to z.shstars14.7k with Windows and posix shells support and various improvements. 10x faster than fasd and autojump, 3x faster than z.shstars14.7k.
  • zabbstars21 - zabb is a command that tries to figure out the shortest memorable abbreviation of a directory that is usable by zstars7.9k to unambiguously jump to that directory.
  • zabrzestars15 - A ZSH abbreviation expansion plugin.
  • zawstars527 - ZSH anything.el-like widget.
  • zcestars60 - Vim’s EasyMotion / Emacs’s ace-jump-mode for ZSH.
  • zcolorsstars44 - Uses your $LS_COLORS to generate a coherent theme for Git and your Zsh prompt, completions and ZSH syntax highlightingstars15.5k.
  • zconveystars1 - Adds ability to send commands to other ZSH sessions, you can use this to cd $PWD on all active ZSH sessions, for example.
  • zedstars6 - A simple wrapper for zstars14.7k to install it via a ZSH plugin.
  • zellij (tranzystorek-io)stars6 - Provides an environment that autostarts zellijstars8.5k as your terminal's multiplexer.
  • zenostars107 - Fuzzy completion and utility plugin powered by Deno.
  • zflaistars1 - A fast logging framework for ZSH.
  • zfzfstars16 - A fzf-powered file picker for ZSH which allows you to quickly navigate the directory hierarchy.
  • zhooksstars38 - Displays the contents of any ZSH hook arrays and the code of any hook functions that have been defined. Useful for debugging.
  • zinfo_linestars1 - Makes more information available to ZSH themes.
  • zinit-annex-bin-gem-nodestars4 - zinitstars1.4k extension that exposes binaries without altering $PATH, installs Ruby gems and Node modules and easily exposes their binaries, and updates the gems and modules when the associated plugin or snippet is updated.
  • zinit-annex-default-icestars1 - Allows user to define ices active for multiple zinit commands.
  • zinit-annex-manstars1 - Zinitstars1.4k extension that generates man pages for all plugins and snippets
  • zinit-annex-meta-pluginsstars1 - Install groups of plugins with a single label (zinitstars1.4k only).
  • zinit-annex-patch-dlstars1 - zinitstars1.4k extension that downloads files and applies patches through the provided dl and patch zinit ices.
  • zinit-annex-readurlstars4 - Adds function to automatically download the newest version of a file to which URL is hosted on a webpage.
  • zinit-annex-ruststars0 - zinitstars1.4k extension that that installs rust and cargo packages inside plugin directories.
  • zinit-annex-teststars0 - zinitstars1.4k extension that runs tests (via make test, for example) – if it finds any of them – after installing and updating a plugin or snippet.
  • zinit-annex-unscopestars2 - Allows installing plugins for zinitstars1.4k without specifying the user name by querying the Github API.
  • zinit-consolestars7 - A semigraphical (curses) consolette for the zinitstars1.4k plugin manager.
  • zinsultsstars6 - Prints insults if a command fails.
  • zjumpstars10 - Simplify ZSH directory navigation; jump to already visited, parent or sub folders.
  • zlitefetchstars6 - Lightweight system information plugin.
  • zoxidestars7.9k - A fast alternative to cd that learns your habits.
  • zsh-selectstars6 - Displays a selection list. It is similar to selecta, but uses the curses library to do display, and when compared to fzfstars47.5k, the main difference is approximate matching instead of fuzzy matching.
  • zsh-z (agkozak)stars1.2k - Jump quickly to directories that you have visited "frecently." A native ZSH port of z.sh - without awk, sed, sort, or date.
  • zsh-z (ptavares)stars0 - Installs and loads zstars14.7k.
  • zshrcstars5 - Load local .zshrc files from your project scopes.
  • zsnapacstars1 - Plugin for taking ZFS pre/post upgrade snapshots on Arch Linux.
  • zsnapshotstars1 - Adds command to dump the current ZSH state into a file, for later restoration by sourcing the snapshot file.
  • ztouchstars1 - Adds touchbar controls for recent history commands, directory stack, cycling between modes and user-mappable commands to the touchbar on macOS.
  • ztracestars7 - Catches output of commands, allows to reuse that output, glue it with history content.
  • zuistars3 - ZSH User Interface library – CGI+DHTML-like rapid TUI application development with ZSH.)

    • Tutorials

    Generic ZSH

  • A Beautifully Productive Terminal Experience - Tutorial using a combination of iTerm 2, ZSH, Preztostars13k, Tmux, and Tmuxinatorstars11.6k to make for an extremely productive developer workflow.
  • Arch Linux's ZSH introduction - Not actually Arch or Linux-specific.
  • GHstars25 - Setup ZSH on debian/Ubuntu-based linuxes. Installs Powerlevel10kstars31.7k, zsh-completionsstars5.6k, zsh-autosuggestionsstars23.7k, fast-syntax-highlightingstars477, and more.
  • How To Make an Awesome Custom Shell with ZSH - A beginner-friendly tutorial on how to install and configure a ZSH shell.
  • commandlinepoweruser.com - Wes Bos' videos introducing ZSH and oh-my-zsh.
  • Outrageously Useful Tips To Master Your Z Shell covers some of the features that ZSH has that Bash doesn't, and using oh-my-zsh.
  • rs-examplestars4 - An example plugin showing how a Rust program can listen to and process commands from ZSH.
  • Why ZSH is Cooler than your Shell - slideshare presentation.
  • xVanjaZstars4 - wrote a quick setup document for oh-my-zsh beginners showing how to use the spaceshipstars17.5k prompt, syntax highlighting at the prompt, autosuggestion and a custom iTerm 2 theme.
  • ZSH for Humansstars995 - A turnkey configuration for ZSH that aims to work really well out of the box. It combines a curated set of ZSH plugins into a coherent whole that feels like a finished product rather than a DIY starter kit.
  • ZSH Ponystars169 - Covers customizing ZSH without a framework.
  • ZSH tips by Christian Schneider - An exhaustive list of ZSH tips by Christian Schneider.

    • Tutorials

    Antigen

  • belak/zsh-utilsstars51 - A minimal set of ZSH plugins designed to be low-friction and low-complexity.
  • mgdm.net/weblog/zsh-antigen/ - Michael Maclean's article about switching from oh-my-zsh to antigen.
  • Oh-my-zsh is the Disease and Antigen is the Vaccine - Josh Davis' introduction to Antigen.

    • Tutorials

    Oh-My-Zsh

  • Getting started with oh-my-zsh - A beginners guide to oh-my-zsh by Dien Bui
  • Learn Zsh in 80 Minutes macOS - A beginners guide to using Oh My Zsh on macOS by Karl Hadwen
  • Oh-My-Zsh! A Work of CLI Magic - Michiel Mulders installation guide for Ubuntu
  • ZSH Gem 24 - Part of the 2011 ZSH Advent Calendar. Covers oh-my-zsh and zshuery.

    • Tutorials

    Prezto

  • A Beautifully Productive Terminal Experience - Mike Buss' blog post about using Prezto, Tmux & Tmuxinator.
  • Migrate from Oh-My-Zsh to Prezto - Jerome Dalbert's blog post on migrating to Prezto.

    • Tutorials

    Zgen

  • rad-shellstars33 - A fantastically feature rich, lightning-fast shell setup, powered by ZSH, Preztostars13k, and Zgenstars1.4k.

    • Tutorials

    Zinit (né zplugin)

  • zinit-configsstars21 - Real-world configuration files (basically a collection of .zshrc files) holding zinitstars1.4k invocations.

    • Tutorials

    superconsole - Windows-only.

  • Choose between clean and inherited environment when starting new SuperConsole sessions
  • Custom colorful scheme, colorful output for various commands
  • Enabled number of ZSH plugins to activate completion, highlighting and history for most comfortable use
  • ssh-agent for git works out-of-box, add your keys to ConEmu/msys64/ConEmu/msys64/home/user/.ssh dir
  • Sets up nano as main editor, enables nano syntax highlighting
  • Custom helper scripts added to ConEmu/msys64/3rdparty

    • Frameworks

    miniplug

  • Only does the bare minimum for managing plugins

    • Frameworks

    sheldon

  • Can manage
    • Any git repository.
      • Branch/tag/commit support.
      • Extra support for GitHub repositories.
      • Extra support for Gists.
    • Arbitrary remote files, simply specify the URL.
    • Local plugins, simply specify the directory path.
  • Highly configurable install methods using handlebars templating.
  • Super-fast parallel installation.
  • Configuration file using TOMLstars17.5k syntax.
  • Uses a lock file for much faster loading of plugins.

    • Frameworks

    zpm

  • Fastest plugin manager (Really, after the first run, zpm will not be used at all)
  • Support for async loading
  • Dependencies between packages

    • Frameworks

    zshing

  • Install ZSH plugins
  • Update ZSH plugins
  • Search by name all available ZSH Plugins
  • Clean unused plugins up

    • Frameworks

    zulu

  • Easily manage your shell environment without editing files.
  • Create aliases, functions and environment variables, and have them available to you at the next shell startup.
  • Add and remove directories from $path, $fpath and $cdpath with simple commands.
  • Install packages, plugins and themes easily, and have them available to you immediately.

    • Setups

    zinit

  • ZPWRstars105 - An extremely powerful custom terminal environment built on top of Zinitstars1.4k for maximum speed. A full terminal configuration framework including zsh, tmux, fzf, vim and spacemacs configurations. It includes:

    • Jan 23rd

    Frameworks

    mzpm

  • Fetching and loading plugins from GitHub
  • Loading local plugins
  • Loading themes

    • Nov 18th, 2021

    Tutorials

    Generic ZSH

  • ZSH Unpluggedstars129 - Good resource if you want to eliminate using a framework but still easily use plugins.

    • Oct 30th, 2021

    Frameworks

    Zinit

  • Turbo mode – 80% faster ZSH startup! for example: instead of 200 ms, it'll be 40 ms
  • Completion management (selectively disable and enable completions)
  • Snippets (↔ regular files downloaded via-URL, e.g.: scripts) and through them Oh My Zsh and Prezto plugins support (→ low overhead)
  • Annexes (↔ Zinit extensions)
  • Reports (from the plugin loads – plugins are no longer black boxes)
  • Plugin unloading (allows e.g.: dynamic theme switching)
  • bindkey capturing and remapping
  • packages
  • Clean fpath (the array $fpath is not being used to add completions and autoload functions, hence it stays concise, not bloated)
  • Services ↔ a single-instance, background plugins
  • Also, in general: all the mechanisms from the ZSH Plugin Standard – Zinit is a reference implementation of the standard.

    • Oct 25th, 2021

    Other Resources

    Other References

  • Mastering ZSHstars888 is a great tutorial that builds on the basics to show you advanced ZSH usage, customizations, and practical examples.

    • Sep 15th, 2021

    Other Resources

    Other References

  • The ZSH Reference Card and zsh-lovers site are indispensable.

    • Feb 7th, 2021

    Setups

    zinit

  • 1.9k+ aliases
  • 330+ git aliases
  • 2.8k line README.md

    • Feb 3rd, 2021

    Frameworks

    znap

  • Multi-repo management
  • Automatic compinit and bashinit - you no longer need them in your .zshrc, znap will do them automatically as needed.

    • Sep 22nd, 2020

    Frameworks

    znap

  • Make any prompt appear instantly. Reduce your startup time from ~200ms to ~40ms with just one command.
  • Asynchronously compile your plugins and functions.
  • Cache those expensive eval $(commands).
  • Clone or pull multiple repos in parallel.
  • Re-clone all your repos without you having to re-enter them.

    • Sep 3rd, 2020

    Setups

    zinit

  • 1 line install

    • Sep 1st, 2018

    Frameworks

    zplug

  • Can manage everything
    • ZSH plugins/UNIX commands on GitHub and Bitbucket
    • Gist files (gist.github.com)
    • Externally managed plugins e.g., oh-my-zshstars151k and preztostars13k plugins/themes
    • Binary artifacts on GitHub Releases
    • Local plugins
    • etc. (you can add your own sources!)
  • Super-fast parallel installation/update
  • Support for lazy-loading
  • Branch/tag/commit support
  • Post-update, post-load hooks
  • Dependencies between packages
  • Interactive interface (fzfstars47.5k, pecostars7.1k, zawstars527, and so on)
  • Cache mechanism for reducing the startup timestars5.2k

    • Aug 14th, 2017

    Installation

    dotzsh

  • Open a new ZSH terminal window or tab

    • May 15th, 2015

    Installation

    Oh-My-Zsh

  • Add the repo to your plugin list

    • Jan 13th, 2015

    Installation

    dotzsh

  • Clone new plugins into .zsh.local/modules

    • Oct 7th, 2014

    Installation

    dotzsh

  • Load the plugin module in .zshrc

    • Installation

    Oh-My-Zsh

  • cd ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins
  • git clone repo

    • Installation

    Prezto

  • Clone the plugin into your prezto modules directory
  • Add the plugin to your .zpreztorc file
  • Open a new terminal window or tab
